query: What is the current state of polling and political dynamics for Zambia's August 2026 presidential election, specifically Hakainde Hichilema's expected vote share? Include recent 2025-2026 polls, opposition coalition strength, whether Edgar Lungu/PF is running (note Lungu died in 2025), and turnout factors.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here is a summary of the key findings on Zambia's August 2026 presidential election:

**Bottom line:** President Hakainde Hichilema (UPND) is the clear favorite, polling in the **mid-50s to low-60s** across credible surveys, against a fragmented, leaderless opposition.

**On the Lungu/PF question (critical for forecasting):** Edgar Lungu **died on 5 June 2025** in Pretoria — and had already been barred by the Constitutional Court in December 2024 from contesting. So Lungu/PF are **not a candidate factor**. The PF is fractured by internal leadership wars, and the opposition mantle has passed to first-time candidate **Brian Mundubile** (Tonse Alliance), with Fred M'membe (Socialist Party) as a secondary force.

**Polling:**
- **ZERN academic survey** (Jun–Nov 2025): Hichilema 55%, opposition 13%, **32% undecided** — the large swing bloc is the biggest uncertainty.
- **Mutombo national poll** (Oct 2025, n=1,009, ±3.1%): Hichilema 60% (58–63%), opposition 35%, 75% approval.
- Reuters (July 2026) calls him favored but facing a "strong challenge" from Mundubile.

**Turnout/context factors:** 8.7M registered voters; strong macroeconomy (6.5% inflation, 4.3% projected growth) but 41% report worsening personal finances; analysts flag **voter apathy** as Hichilema's main risk; and democratic-backsliding concerns (delimitation/gerrymandering, Cyber Crimes Act, shortened voter-register inspection, the Lungu burial dispute) could shape perceptions of fairness.

The full markdown report, structured data points, and all sources have been delivered via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.snippet: 55% plan to vote for the president, 13% for opposition parties, 32% don't know/refused. Fieldwork June-November 2025.

sources.snippet: Dr Namuunda Mutombo poll: Hichilema 60% (58-63%) vs 35% opposition; 75% approval; n=1,009; +/-3.1%; Oct 2-15 2025.

sources.title: Elections 2026: is Zambia's democratic success story beginning to fray?
sources.snippet: Record 26 presidential candidates registered (14 approved); delimitation adds 70 constituencies; voter-register inspection cut 90->14 days; 8.7M registered voters.

sources.snippet: 26 presidential candidates cleared, 184 independents; main challengers Brian Mundubile (Tonse Alliance) and Fred M'membe (Socialist Party).

sources.title: Zambia 2026: Can Lungu revive a divided opposition to challenge Hichilema?
sources.snippet: PF fractured by leadership changes (Sampa, Chabinga); Lungu disqualified Dec 2024 and led Tonse Alliance before his death.

sources.snippet: Provincial breakdown: UPND Southern 74%, Western 68%, NW 66%; Mundubile/NRPUP Luapula 76%; regional split. Methodology unverified.

sources.title: The Biggest Opposition to Hichilema May Be Voter Apathy, Not the Opposition Parties
sources.snippet: Analysis arguing voter apathy and mobilization, not opposition strength, is the main challenge for UPND in 2026.

sources.title: Zambia: The Challenges of Maintaining Democratic Progress
sources.snippet: Hichilema won 59% in 2021 galvanized by strong youth turnout; seeking second term; country of 22 million.

key_facts: Zambia's general elections are set for 13 August 2026; a presidential candidate needs 50%+1 to win in the first round.
Edgar Lungu died 5 June 2025 in Pretoria, South Africa, at age 68 — he is NOT a 2026 candidate, and had already been barred by the Constitutional Court (Dec 2024) from contesting.
Hichilema won the 2021 election with 59% of the vote, unseating Lungu, and is seeking a second term as incumbent (UPND).
ZERN academic survey (fieldwork Jun-Nov 2025): 55% intend to vote Hichilema, 13% opposition, 32% don't know/refused — large undecided/swing bloc.
Dr. Namuunda Mutombo national poll (fieldwork 2-15 Oct 2025, n=1,009, ±3.1%, all 10 provinces): Hichilema 60% (58-63% range) vs 35% opposition; 75% approval rating.
Reuters/CNBC (9 July 2026): Hichilema favored to win but faces a 'strong challenge' from opposition leader Brian Mundubile (Tonse Alliance).
The Patriotic Front (PF) is fractured after leadership wars (Sampa vs Chabinga) and Lungu's death; opposition has no single dominant vehicle.

Brian Mundubile (55, lawyer, ex-MP) is the leading opposition consensus candidate, running for the first time; Fred M'membe (Socialist Party) is a secondary challenger arrested March 2026 under the Cyber Crimes Act.
Record opposition fragmentation: reports of 14-26 presidential candidates cleared/registered and 184 independents on the ballot.
8.7 million registered voters (of 21M+ population); 86% of Zambians say elections are the best way to choose leaders (Afrobarometer).
Economy is a double-edged issue: inflation ~6.5% (lowest in 8+ years), IMF 4.3% 2026 GDP growth, $6.5B reserves, restructured debt — but ZERN found 41% say personal finances worsened vs 38% improved.
Analysts identify voter apathy (demobilization of Hichilema's 2021 coalition) as the biggest risk to the incumbent, not opposition strength.

Democratic-backsliding concerns: 2025 Cyber Crimes Act, delimitation adding 70 constituencies (Southern Province seats 20->29, gerrymandering claims), voter-register inspection cut 90->14 days, Constitutional Amendment No.13 of 2025.
A prolonged Lungu burial dispute (family vs government; South African court ruled 8 Aug 2025 for Zambian govt) has fueled anti-government sentiment.
Regional/ethnic voting split persists: UPND strong in Southern/Western/North-Western provinces; opposition (Mundubile) strong in Northern/Eastern/Luapula/Muchinga.
